perturbation.original_text: Each party undertakes in respect of Confidential Information fo r which it is the recipient:‐ ሺaሻ to treat such Confidential Information disclosed by the dis closing party as confidential; ሺbሻ not without the disclosing party's prior written consent in each case to communicate or disclose any part of such Confidential Informati on to any person except:‐
perturbation.changed_text: Each party undertakes in respect of Confidential Information for which it is the recipient:‐ ሺaሻ to treat such Confidential Information disclosed by the disclosing party as confidential; ሺbሻ may communicate or disclose any part of such Confidential Information to any person, without the disclosing party's consent:
perturbation.explanation: The change replaces 'not without the disclosing party's prior written consent' with 'may communicate or disclose any part of such Confidential Information to any person, without the disclosing party's consent'. This directly contradicts the core purpose of an NDA which is to limit the disclosure of confidential information. It creates a loophole where a receiving party is free to disclose information without consent, conflicting with section 3(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party undertakes in respect of Confidential Information fo r which it is the recipient:‐ ሺaሻ to treat such Confidential Information disclosed by the dis closing party as confidential; ሺbሻ may communicate or disclose any part of such Confidential Information to any person, without the disclosing party's consent:
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section (b) to state that a party 'may communicate or disclose any part of such Confidential Information to any person, without the disclosing party's consent'. This directly contradicts the core principle of confidentiality agreements, which typically restrict such disclosure. The contradicted text being the exact same as the Changed Text only highlights the level of the contradiction, as it is within the same clause. This is a strong and clear contradiction, and would cause confusion and likely unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of confidentiali ty in Clause 3 above shall not apply:‐ ሺ a ሻ t o a n y p o r t i o n o f C o n f i d e n t i a l I n f o r m a t i o n w h e r e t h e r e c i p i ent can demonstrate that the Confidential Information concerned:‐ ሺ i ሻ i s o r h a s b e c o m e p u b l i c l y k n o w n t h r o u g h n o f a u l t o f t h e recipient, its employees, agents and sub‐contractors; or ሺiiሻ is lawfully received from an independent third party witho ut any restriction and without any obligation of confidentiality; or ሺiiiሻ is already known to the recipient with no obligation of confidentiality at the date it was disclosed by or obtained fro m the disclosing party; or ሺivሻ is disclosed without restri ction by the disclosing party t o any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of confidentiali ty in Clause 3 above shall not apply:‐ ሺ a ሻ t o a n y p o r t i o n o f C o n f i d e n t i a l I n f o r m a t i o n w h e r e t h e r e c i p i ent can demonstrate that the Confidential Information concerned:‐ ሺ i ሻ i s o r h a s b e c o m e p u b l i c l y k n o w n t h r o u g h n o f a u l t o f t h e recipient, its employees, agents and sub‐contractors; or ሺiiሻ is lawfully received from an independent third party witho ut any restriction and without any obligation of confidentiality; or ሺiiiሻ is already known to the recipient with no obligation of confidentiality at the date it was disclosed by or obtained fro m the disclosing party; or ሺivሻ is disclosed without restri ction by the disclosing party t o any third party. However, this entire clause does not apply, if disclosing party informs receiving party to do not consider this clause valid
perturbation.explanation: By adding the sentence 'However, this entire clause does not apply, if disclosing party informs receiving party to do not consider this clause valid' to Clause 4, we create a contradiction. Clause 4 originally specified the conditions under which confidentiality obligations *do not* apply. The added sentence permits the disclosing party to unilaterally invalidate the *entire* clause, which removes those exceptions. This contradicts the initially stated exceptions and now creates obligations of confidentiality on items which would have been initially excluded from such.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of confidentiali ty in Clause 3 above shall not apply:‐ ሺ a ሻ t o a n y p o r t i o n o f C o n f i d e n t i a l I n f o r m a t i o n w h e r e t h e r e c i p i ent can demonstrate that the Confidential Information concerned:‐ ሺ i ሻ i s o r h a s b e c o m e p u b l i c l y k n o w n t h r o u g h n o f a u l t o f t h e recipient, its employees, agents and sub‐contractors; or ሺiiሻ is lawfully received from an independent third party witho ut any restriction and without any obligation of confidentiality; or ሺiiiሻ is already known to the recipient with no obligation of confidentiality at the date it was disclosed by or obtained fro m the disclosing party; or ሺivሻ is disclosed without restri ction by the disclosing party t o any third party. However, this entire clause does not apply, if disclosing party informs receiving party to do not consider this clause valid
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by allowing the disclosing party to invalidate the entire clause regarding exceptions to confidentiality. The original text lists specific situations where confidentiality doesn't apply (e.g., information already public). The added sentence in the changed text gives the disclosing party the power to remove these exceptions, creating a direct conflict with the original terms of the exceptions and the contradicted text, causing uncertainty and unenforceability of those specific exceptions.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the completion or termination of the Business Purpose, eac h recipient shall promptly deliver up to the disclosing party all Materials supplied by the disclosing party incorporating any Confidential Information of that party and all copies thereof and destroy or erase any Confidential Informatio n contained in any materials and documentation prepared by or on behalf of the recipient or recorded in any memory device. Within fourteen ሺ14ሻ days of suc h request or completion of the Business Purpose the recipient shall certify in writing to the disclosing party that it has fully complied with its obligation s under this Clause. Notwithstanding the foregoing each recipient may retain one cop y of all Materials containing Confidential Information of the disclosing party received or made in connection with this Agreement for archival purposes only, subject always to strict compliance with the obligations of Clauses 3 and 5.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the completion or termination of the Business Purpose, eac h recipient shall promptly deliver up to the disclosing party all Materials supplied by the disclosing party incorporating any Confidential Information of that party and all copies thereof and destroy or erase any Confidential Informatio n contained in any materials and documentation prepared by or on behalf of the recipient or recorded in any memory device. Within fourteen ሺ14ሻ days of suc h request or completion of the Business Purpose the recipient shall certify in writing to the disclosing party that it has fully complied with its obligation s under this Clause. Notwithstanding the foregoing each recipient must retain one copy of all Materials containing Confidential Information of the disclosing party received or made in connection with this Agreement indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'may retain one copy of all Materials' to 'must retain one copy of all Materials' creates a contradiction with the original instruction to 'destroy or erase any Confidential Information'. The Receiving party is now obligated to both destroy all confidential information while simultaneusly retaining a copy of the materials which would be against the clause above that states that they should fully comply with its obligation s under this Clause. The change introduces internal inconsistency, making the enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the completion or termination of the Business Purpose, eac h recipient shall promptly deliver up to the disclosing party all Materials supplied by the disclosing party incorporating any Confidential Information of that party and all copies thereof and destroy or erase any Confidential Informatio n contained in any materials and documentation prepared by or on behalf of the recipient or recorded in any memory device. Within fourteen ሺ14ሻ days of suc h request or completion of the Business Purpose the recipient shall certify in writing to the disclosing party that it has fully complied with its obligation s under this Clause. Notwithstanding the foregoing each recipient must retain one copy of all Materials containing Confidential Information of the disclosing party received or made in connection with this Agreement indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'may retain one copy' to 'must retain one copy', which directly contradicts the initial obligation to 'destroy or erase any Confidential Information'. The Contradicted Text mirrors the Changed Text. This creates a direct conflict because the recipient is now obligated to both destroy and retain the same confidential information. This adheres to the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition and it is a strong in-text contradiction.
